data.narrative:
Flock Safety, a Series E defense and security company founded by Garrett Langley, Matt Feury, and Paige Todd, carries a current post-money valuation of $7.5B following $950M in cumulative fundraising. The peer universe comprises four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Saronic at $9.2B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B, Skydio at $2.2B, and Epirus at $1.4B), supplemented by a broader defense-tech ecosystem including public comparables DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $7.5B last post-money and applying a 1.4x Series E step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $10.5B. Peer-median methodology across the four same-sector comparables yields a median of $3.4B, with a range spanning $1.4B to $9.2B. The divergence between methods reflects material variance in valuation outcomes across the peer set.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $2.8B (low) to $12.1B (high), with a median of $6.9B. The current $7.5B valuation sits near the midpoint of this range, though the wide spread—spanning stage-progression upside of $10.5B against peer-median downside of $3.4B—reflects genuine uncertainty around appropriate valuation multiples in this sector. Key unknowns include relative growth rates and profitability trajectories versus the peer cohort, where valuation dispersion is substantial.
